free_text: Measurement of the cross section of the residues from
the 11B-induced reaction on 89Y and 93Nb: Production
of 97Ru and 101mRh
Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee
BARC-TIFR Pelletron facility
Self-supporting pure (99.99%) natural yttrium and
niobium foils of metal having thicknesses were lies
between 0.84-2.9 mg/cm2 and 1.2-2.1 mg/cm2,
respectively. The Al-backing foils had thickness
between 1.5-2 mg/cm2. Annular aluminum holders with
an inner and outer diameter 12 and 20 mm,
respectively, were used to mount the foils.
A broad energy germanium (BEGe) based detector
attached with a PC operating with the GENIE-2K
software. The energy resolution of the detector was
better than 2.0 keV at 1332 keV energy.
A total of 4-5 stacks was irradiated
individually for each target-projectile combination
varying the incident energy of 11B with a slight
overlap between them.
The total error in the cross section
measurement are:
Uncertainty due to nonuniformity of samples and
in measuring its thickness (~5%)
Uncertainty due to fluctuation of beam current
Uncertainty due to efficiency calibration of
the detector (~2%)
Theoretical calculations performed with PACE4
and EMPIRE3.2.
Gulshan Premi and VT
